description_long: This object consists of two commercial passenger airplanes positioned side by side, each featuring a streamlined fuselage, swept-back wings, and a conventional tail assembly. The left airplane has a light gray body with a blue vertical stabilizer, while the right airplane displays a similar gray body with a gold vertical stabilizer and tail section. Both airplanes are equipped with two underwing turbofan engines and tricycle landing gear, with the main gear under the wings and a nose gear at the front. The cockpits are located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows visible along the sides for passengers. The wings are low-mounted and exhibit a slight dihedral angle, and the overall design is typical of modern narrow-body commercial jets.
description: A pair of modern commercial airplanes with swept-back low wings, underwing turbofan engines, and distinct blue and gold tail sections. Both feature forward cockpits, tricycle landing gear, and streamlined fuselages.
description_view_0: Front view showing the nose, cockpit windows, and the underwing engines of both airplanes, with blue and gold tail fins visible.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the length of the fuselage, the low-mounted swept wings, and the color difference between the blue and gold tail sections.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, the dihedral angle of the wings, and the contrasting tail colors.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again showing the fuselage, wing configuration, and the distinct blue and gold tail sections.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with swept-back wings and slight dihedral angle; no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Twin turbofan underwing engines on each airplane.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units under the wings and a single nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, typical of commercial airliners.
